title: Weeknight Bolognese
description: From Barefoot Contessa Foolproof — a rich, deeply flavored Bolognese that comes together in just 25 minutes. The secret is a splash of white wine, a bit of heavy cream, and freshly grated nutmeg. Ina serves it over pappardelle for maximum sauce-clinging surface area. This is weeknight dinner at its finest.

ingredients.item: Whole peeled plum tomatoes (28-oz can), crushed by hand
Freshly grated Parmesan cheese, for serving

instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a large (12-inch) skillet over medium-high heat. Add the garlic and red pepper flakes and cook for 1 minute. Add the ground beef and cook, breaking it up with a wooden spoon, until no longer pink, about 5 minutes.
Add the wine and cook for 5 to 6 minutes, until most of the wine has evaporated. Add the tomatoes, tomato paste, salt, and pepper. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for 10 minutes.
Stir in the heavy cream and nutmeg. Taste and adjust seasonings.
Meanwhile, cook the pasta in a large pot of boiling salted water according to package directions. Reserve 1 cup of pasta water before draining.
Toss the drained pasta with the Bolognese sauce, adding a splash of pasta water if needed to loosen the sauce. Serve topped with fresh basil and grated Parmesan.
tips: The heavy cream and nutmeg are what make this taste like a proper Bolognese in 25 minutes — don't skip them.
Pappardelle is ideal because the wide ribbons hold the chunky sauce beautifully.
Crushing the canned tomatoes by hand gives a better texture than using a food processor.
Source: Barefoot Contessa Foolproof by Ina Garten (2012)
